frameworks.name: activity flow mapping
frameworks.one_line: empirically derived network model predicting regional activations from connectivity-weighted activity

frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: intrinsic baseline network organization used as prediction reference

frameworks.name: task-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: task-induced reconfiguration of inter-regional relationships added on top of intrinsic networks

core_question: Do small task-related changes in functional connectivity carry functional relevance for shaping cognitive task activations beyond resting-state connectivity?
key_assumptions: activity flow mapping links connectivity to activations
resting-state FC indexes intrinsic network organization
task activations across 360 cortical regions are valid prediction targets
manipulations: connectivity source: resting-state vs task-state vs task-general
24 cognitive task conditions across 360 cortical regions
key_findings: task-state FC improved prediction of independent task activations across all 24 conditions and 360 regions
individual-specific FC patterns strongly drove prediction accuracy
task-general FC from other tasks still improved predictions beyond resting-state FC
interpretations: task-related FC changes dynamically reshape brain network organization
small task-induced connectivity shifts redirect the flow of neural activity during task performance
